title: `check` does not appear to detect many cases of multiple drivers
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Verilog code:
```verilog
module top(inout a, input b);
  always @(*) a <= 1'b0;
  always @(*) a <= b ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;
  // or:
  // always @(*) a <= b;
endmodule
```
Yosys script:
```
proc
check -assert
```

## Expected behavior

`check` would log an error.

## Actual behavior

```
3. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module top..
found and reported 0 problems.
```

## Notes

`opt_clean` prints a warning for the uncommmented case but not the commented one.

Based on my understanding of IEEE 1364.1-2002, both of the cases above are not legal synthesizable Verilog:
![screenshot_20180504_171527](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/39641728-ce7dd5a4-4fbe-11e8-8b90-da98304c12a4.png)
code_blocks:
3. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module top..
found and reported 0 problems.
